--- title: "Homework I: Graph Theory" --- ## Vertex and edge sets ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![A simple graph.](homework1-answered_files/figure-html/fig-grex1-1.png){#fig-grex1 width=960} ::: ::: Consider the graph shown in @fig-grex1: 1. Write down the **vertex set** of the graph:

A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H, I, J, K


VA, VB, VC, VD, VE, VF, VG, VH, VI, VJ, VK 1. Write down the **edge set** of the graph:
::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | |:---|:---|:---|:---|:---|:---|:---|:---|:---|:---|:---|:---|:---|:---|:---|:---|:---|:---|:---| |AC, |BC, |AE, |CF, |DF, |AG, |BG, |EG, |AH, |CH, |FH, |GH, |BI, |CI, |BJ, |HJ, |BK, |GK, |HK, | ::: ::: ## Node Neighborhoods 1. Write down the **neighborhood** of node **C**
A, B, F, H, I
1. Write down the **neighborhood** of node **K**
B, G, H
1. What is the **intersection** of the neighborhoods of nodes **C** and **K**?
B, H
1. What is the **intersection** of the neighborhoods of nodes **G** and **H**?
A, K
1. What is the **union** of the neighborhoods of nodes **F** and **A**?
C, D, H, E, G
## Graph Metrics 1. What is the **order** of the graph?
11
1. What is the **size** of the graph?
19
1. Write down the graph's **degree sequence**: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} | H| B| C| G| A| F| K| E| I| J| D| |--:|--:|--:|--:|--:|--:|--:|--:|--:|--:|--:| | 6| 5| 5| 5| 4| 3| 3| 2| 2| 2| 1| ::: ::: 1. Write down the graph's **maximum Degree**:
6
1. Write down the graph's **minimum degree**:
1
1. What is the graph's **degree range**?
5
1. What is the graph's **sum of degrees**?
38
1. What is the graph's **average degree**?
3.455
1. What is the graph's **maximum size**?
55
1. Compute the **density** of the graph:
0.345
## Subgraphs 1. Go back to @fig-grex1. Draw the **node-deleted** subgraph of this graph that *excludes* nodes *F*, and *H*. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![A node deleted graph.](homework1-answered_files/figure-html/fig-grex2-1.png){#fig-grex2 width=960} ::: ::: 1. List the **isolate** nodes in the node-deleted subgraph you obtained in the previous step.
D
